workflow_rationale: This is a greenfield enhancement that replaces rule-based recommendation generation with AI-powered analysis using Claude. It builds on existing infrastructure (database schema, API routes, AI clients) while introducing a new AI analysis capability that transforms raw visibility metrics into actionable, prioritized recommendations. This is a key competitive differentiator requiring multiple implementation layers (AI logic, API enhancement, database persistence).

phases.name: AI Recommendation Analysis Logic
phases.description: Build the core Claude AI integration for analyzing brand visibility data and generating prioritized recommendations with impact scores

subtasks.description: Create AI recommendation generator with Claude integration

key_requirements: Design system prompt requesting JSON output with recommendation structure
Extract text from response.content blocks (filter type === 'text')
Manual JSON.parse() - no native structured output
Handle API errors with try-catch and retry logic (max 3 attempts)

recommendations.category: technical_seo | content_optimization | schema_markup | citation_building | brand_consistency | competitor_analysis | content_freshness | authority_building
recommendations.title: string (concise)

prompt_design_guidelines: Context: Explain you're analyzing GEO visibility data
Output format: JSON array matching recommendation structure
Include in system prompt: 'Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown'

subtasks.notes: Created src/lib/ai/recommendations.ts with full Claude AI integration for recommendation generation. Implemented: generateAIRecommendations function, VisibilityData types, AIRecommendationOutput types, retry logic with exponential backoff, response parsing with validation, impact scoring algorithm, and deduplication logic. TypeScript compilation verified with no errors in the new file.

subtasks.description: Create TypeScript types for AI recommendation input/output

implementation_notes.import_existing_enums: Import category, priority, impact, effort enums from @/lib/db/schema/recommendations

subtasks.notes: Created src/lib/ai/types/recommendation-input.ts with comprehensive TypeScript type definitions for AI recommendation input/output. Includes: AI platform types (AIPlatform, SentimentType, GapSeverity), recommendation enum types aligned with database schema, visibility data input types (PlatformVisibility, ContentGap, CompetitorMetrics, VisibilityData), AI recommendation output types (AIRecommendationOutput, GeneratedRecommendation), generation result types (TokenUsage, GenerationResult, GenerationSummary, GenerationApiResponse), deduplication types, generation options types, and impact scoring types. TypeScript compilation verified with no errors in the new file.

impact_calculation: Platform reach (weight: 0.4) - How many platforms affected
Visibility gap severity (weight: 0.3) - Current vs target mention rate
Expected traffic gain (weight: 0.2) - Estimated percentage increase

post_processing: Filter out recommendations with impact score < 30

subtasks.notes: Implemented recommendation impact scoring algorithm with weighted factors following patterns from priority-scoring.ts. Added: IMPACT_SCORING_WEIGHTS config, calculatePlatformReachScore, calculateVisibilityGapScore, calculateTrafficGainScore, calculateCompetitiveAdvantageScore, extractImpactFactors, calculateWeightedImpactScore, determinePriorityFromScore, and enhanced calculateRecommendationImpactScore. Created comprehensive test suite with 62 passing tests covering all scoring functions and integration scenarios.

phases.name: Database Persistence Layer
phases.description: Create Drizzle ORM queries for storing and retrieving AI-generated recommendations in PostgreSQL

subtasks.description: Create Drizzle query functions for recommendation CRUD

required_functions: getRecommendationsByBrand(brandId: string, filters?: { status?, priority?, category? }) => Promise<Recommendation[]>
key_patterns: Use eq(), and(), desc() from drizzle-orm for conditions
JSONB steps field: no special handling needed (array of strings)
Map AI output to schema: { category, priority, impact, effort, title, description, steps, source: 'monitoring' }
implementation_notes.enum_validation: Ensure category, priority, impact, effort match pgEnum definitions exactly

subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive CRUD query functions in src/lib/db/queries/recommendations.ts. Includes: createRecommendation, createRecommendations (batch), getRecommendationById, getRecommendations (with filters/pagination/sort), getRecommendationsByBrandId, getRecommendationsByBrandIds, getRecommendationsByAuditId, getPendingRecommendations, countRecommendationsByStatus, countRecommendationsByPriority, updateRecommendation, updateRecommendationStatus, assignRecommendation, bulkUpdateStatus, deleteRecommendation, deleteRecommendations, deleteRecommendationsByBrandId, deleteDismissedRecommendations, findSimilarRecommendation. TypeScript verification passed. Committed as 66c506f5.

subtasks.description: Add duplicate detection logic for recommendations

verification.instructions: Test with duplicate recommendation titles - should skip insertion

duplicate_detection_strategy: Query existing recommendations for brandId with status != 'dismissed'
Compare new recommendation titles with existing (case-insensitive, trimmed)
Fuzzy match: title similarity > 80% (Levenshtein or simple substring check)
Skip duplicates, return count of skipped in metadata
Log duplicates for debugging: console.log('Skipped duplicate:', title)
implementation_notes.edge_case: If ALL recommendations are duplicates, return empty array with warning message

subtasks.notes:
Added comprehensive duplicate detection logic:
- DuplicateHandling type with "skip", "error", "allow" modes
- createRecommendation now supports optional duplicate detection
- createRecommendationsWithDuplicateDetection for batch operations with automatic filtering
- Helper functions: findExistingDuplicates, buildDuplicateKey, isDuplicateRecommendation, checkRecommendationDuplicate
- Duplicate detection based on brandId + title (case-insensitive) + category

phases.name: API Endpoint Enhancement
phases.description: Modify the existing /api/recommendations/generate endpoint to use AI-powered analysis instead of rule-based generation

subtasks.description: Update generate endpoint to call AI recommendation logic

changes_required: Replace call to rule-based generateRecommendations() with AI version
Transform existing MonitorData/AuditData into VisibilityData format for AI
Call insertRecommendations() to persist AI-generated results to DB
Return both generated recommendations AND database IDs in response

data_transformation.from_monitor_data: Extract platform mention rates, sentiment scores, competitor metrics
data_transformation.from_audit_data: Extract content gaps (missing schema types, low scores, identified issues)
data_transformation.to_visibility_data: Combine into single VisibilityData object with platforms, contentGaps, competitorData arrays

error_handling: AI API failures: Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)
Invalid JSON from Claude: Log error, return user-friendly message
Database insert failures: Return 503 Service Unavailable
Insufficient visibility data: Return empty array with 200 status + warning message

response_format.recommendations: array of recommendations with DB IDs

subtasks.notes:
Updated generate endpoint to call AI recommendation logic. Changes include:
- Added useAI flag to request schema (defaults to true)
- Imported generateAIRecommendations from @/lib/ai/recommendations
- Imported createRecommendationsWithDuplicateDetection for DB persistence
- Added transformToVisibilityData function to convert MonitorData/AuditData into VisibilityData format
- Call AI recommendation generator when useAI is true
- Persist AI-generated recommendations to database with duplicate detection
- Enhanced response with database IDs, token usage, persisted count, and duplicates skipped
- Added helper functions: normalizePlatformName, mapIssueSeverityToGapSeverity, getPlatformsAffectedByCategory, mapAIRecToResponse
- Rule-based generation fallback still available via useAI: false
Committed as 0d5f117d.

subtasks.description: Add input validation for generate endpoint

fields.useAI: z.boolean().default(true) - flag to use AI vs rule-based

error_responses.404: Brand not found or doesn't belong to user's org
error_responses.500: Internal server error (AI or DB failures)

subtasks.notes: Added input validation for generate endpoint: explicit handling for empty/invalid JSON bodies with 400 response, standardized error response format with success: false field matching other API endpoints.

subtasks.description: Add observability logging for AI operations

verification.instructions: Check console logs during generation - should show prompt length, response time, token usage

logging_points: Before AI call: Log prompt length, brandId, timestamp
After AI call: Log response time, token usage (from response.usage), recommendations count
On error: Log error type, message, stack trace
On duplicate detection: Log skipped count
On database insert: Log persisted count
implementation_notes.log_format: console.log('[AI Recommendations]', { brandId, promptLength, responseTime, tokensUsed, count })
implementation_notes.production_consideration: Replace console.log with structured logging library (e.g., pino, winston) in future

subtasks.notes:
Added structured observability logging for AI operations. Logs include:
- Request/generation start with input context
- Prompt length metrics (user prompt, system prompt, total)
- AI response time in milliseconds
- Token usage (input, output, total)
- Retry attempts with backoff timing
- Success/failure status with priority breakdown
- Full request timing from start to completion

All logs use structured format with prefixes [AI:Recommendations] and [API:Recommendations/Generate] for easy filtering.

phases.name: Testing & Validation
phases.description: Write comprehensive tests for AI recommendation logic, database operations, and API endpoints

subtasks.description: Write unit tests for AI recommendation generator

test_cases: Impact scoring correctly prioritizes critical vs low recommendations
Empty visibility data returns empty array without errors
AI API failure triggers retry logic and returns graceful error
Invalid JSON from Claude is caught and handled
Recommendations filtered by minimum impact threshold
Duplicate recommendations are detected and deduplicated

mocking_strategy.claude_client: Mock getClaudeClient() to return fake responses
mocking_strategy.test_data: Create fixture visibility data with known gaps for predictable results

subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive unit test suite tests/unit/ai-recommendations.test.ts with 65 tests covering:
- generateAIRecommendations function: valid generation, empty/missing input handling, impact threshold filtering, max recommendations limiting, sorting by impact score, API failure with retry logic, invalid JSON handling, markdown code blocks, field validation/normalization, skipping incomplete recommendations
- Impact scoring functions: calculatePlatformReachScore, calculateVisibilityGapScore, calculateTrafficGainScore, calculateCompetitiveAdvantageScore, extractImpactFactors, calculateWeightedImpactScore, determinePriorityFromScore, calculateRecommendationImpactScore
- deduplicateRecommendations: unique handling, exact duplicates, case-insensitive matching, fuzzy matching (>80% similarity), existing title filtering, title normalization

All 65 tests pass. Committed as ad1a3b4d.

subtasks.description: Write integration tests for generate API endpoint

test_scenarios: POST /api/recommendations/generate with valid data returns 200 + recommendations
POST with malformed body returns 400 + validation errors
Recommendations are actually inserted into database (verify with query)
Duplicate generation request skips existing recommendations

test_setup.database: Use test database or in-memory SQLite for isolation
test_setup.authentication: Mock auth() to return test userId and orgId
test_setup.cleanup: afterEach() hook to delete test recommendations

subtasks.notes: Created tests/integration/recommendations-api.test.ts with 28 comprehensive tests covering: authentication (401 unauthorized), validation errors (400 bad request), brand not found (404), successful AI generation (200), rule-based generation fallback, error handling, and content validation. All tests pass using vi.doMock() pattern for proper mock isolation.

subtasks.description: Manual QA testing with real visibility data

verification.instructions: Use Postman to POST real brand visibility data to /api/recommendations/generate and verify recommendations are specific, actionable, and correctly prioritized

test_data_sources: Use existing brandMentions data from database
Use real audit results if available
Create synthetic test case with extreme gaps (0% mention rate on ChatGPT)
validation_checklist: Recommendations reference specific AI platforms (not generic)
Steps are actionable (concrete actions, not vague)
Priority aligns with gap severity (low mention rate = critical)
Categories match gap type (missing schema = schema_markup)
No hallucinations (recommendations based on actual data)

subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive QA testing documentation (qa-testing-manual.md) with 8 test scenarios covering: successful AI generation, empty data handling, invalid brand ID, missing validation, unauthorized access, duplicate detection, rule-based fallback, and performance. Fixed lint issues in recommendations.ts and test file. Committed as 0acee130.

phases.name: End-to-End Integration
phases.description: Verify the complete flow from API request through AI generation to database persistence and retrieval

steps: 1. Start dev server: npm run dev
5. Verify recommendations from step 2 appear in GET response
6. Verify database contains persisted recommendations (check via Drizzle Studio)

success_criteria: Recommendations persisted with all required fields

subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive E2E workflow validation test suite (tests/e2e/recommendations-workflow.test.ts) with 16 tests covering:

- Complete workflow: generate → persist → retrieve → deduplicate
- Data integrity across multiple generation cycles
- Response structure validation (summary, sources, token usage, grouped priorities)
- Recommendation quality validation (platform-specific, actionable steps, priority levels, expected outcomes, timeframes)
- Error handling (AI service unavailability, database failures, empty visibility data)
- Performance metrics (timestamps, token tracking)

All 109 tests pass (65 unit + 28 integration + 16 E2E):
- tests/unit/ai-recommendations.test.ts: 65 tests
- tests/integration/recommendations-api.test.ts: 28 tests
- tests/e2e/recommendations-workflow.test.ts: 16 tests

Fixed TypeScript type issue in recommendations-api.test.ts for mock AI response spread types.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution required due to dependencies
parallelism.reasoning: Phase 2 depends on Phase 1 (needs AI types), Phase 3 depends on Phase 2 (needs DB queries), Phase 4 depends on Phase 3 (tests the API), Phase 5 depends on Phase 4 (E2E integration). No parallelization possible.

acceptance_criteria: All unit tests pass (AI recommendation logic, impact scoring)
All integration tests pass (API endpoints, database persistence)
Manual QA confirms recommendations are platform-specific and actionable
Generation completes within 30 seconds for typical datasets
Error handling works for AI API failures and invalid data
Existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)

verification_steps.name: Existing Tests
verification_steps.expected_outcome: No regressions - all existing tests pass

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk due to core business logic affecting decision-making. Requires thorough unit and integration testing but no security-sensitive operations. No staging deployment needed for development phase.

flows: Generate recommendations → Verify persistence → Retrieve recommendations

checks: Steps field is valid JSONB array
Category, priority, impact, effort enums are valid
